title: Process-first target contract for Compliance Review, Block and Release

source_definition.task: Define target surfaces, state machine, role guards, payload visibility, audit/evidence requirements and acceptance criteria for Compliance Review, Block and Release.
definition_of_done: Acceptance criteria cover positive and negative paths.
UI, service and test boundaries are named.

contract_decision.reason: DOMAIN-G already has route and runtime seams; EPIC-11 should close proof and contract debt instead of adding parallel compliance surfaces.

target_surfaces.primary_job: Triage one compliance review item and open the scoped decision room without release, export or client-acceptance overclaim.
target_surfaces.viewport_rule: Queue, selected review context, blocker and one primary next action must fit without page-scroll in the target operating viewport.

target_surfaces.primary_job: Evaluate advisor approval, evidence, payload safety, permission and audit readiness before choosing release, block or evidence request.
target_surfaces.viewport_rule: Decision context, preconditions, blocker, audit readiness and one command slot must be scannable without burying the primary action.

target_surfaces.primary_job: Confirm a release only after all release preconditions pass and the audited command can persist the release state.
target_surfaces.viewport_rule: Confirmation stays command-focused; policy detail and proof summaries remain concise.

target_surfaces.primary_job: Block release or request evidence with reason and audit while preserving release/client/export denial.
target_surfaces.viewport_rule: Block reason, requested evidence, owner, due date and release-denial state stay above the proof details.

target_surfaces.primary_job: Inspect persisted review, block, release and exception events without treating audit display as the mutation source.
target_surfaces.viewport_rule: Audit route is a scan-and-inspect surface, not a long proof wall; persisted AuditEvent references remain the source of truth.

role_guards.hard_negative: Wrong role, tenant or object scope must deny queue mutation without payload leak.
Missing advisor approval, evidence, client-safe payload, permission or audit readiness blocks release.
Requesting evidence cannot create evidence sufficiency, release, export readiness or client visibility.
Block release must clear release state and keep client/export projection denied.
Compliance release cannot bypass release spine, audit persistence, payload safety or client-acceptance separation.

payload_visibility_policy.client_safe_payload: Only the compliance-released projection may become client-visible. Release is still not client acceptance.
payload_visibility_policy.internal_payload: Internal draft text, internal rationale, analyst notes, advisor notes, compliance notes, audit debug, process command debug and raw evidence payload remain internal.
payload_visibility_policy.audit_payload: Operational UI may show concise audit references and event outcomes, but persisted AuditEvent records are the audit source of truth.

acceptance_processes.positive: Compliance queue produces one scoped compliance review item and can open the correct decision room.
acceptance_processes.negative: Queue visibility cannot release, export, block, mutate client visibility or leak internal payload.

acceptance_processes.positive: Precondition check returns pass/fail for advisor approval, evidence, payload, permission, audit and release-spine readiness.
acceptance_processes.negative: Any missing precondition keeps release blocked and records no client-safe release.

acceptance_processes.positive: Evidence completeness requires reviewed, scoped, current and client-safe evidence for the specific gate.
acceptance_processes.negative: Upload success, stale evidence, unlinked evidence or internal-only evidence cannot satisfy release.

acceptance_processes.positive: Compliance can request evidence with reason, target ownership and audit while release remains blocked.
acceptance_processes.negative: Evidence request cannot be treated as sufficiency, release, export or client visibility.

acceptance_processes.positive: Compliance can block release with reason and audit and keep release/client/export state denied.
acceptance_processes.negative: Block cannot become client rejection, client acceptance, export action or hidden release.

acceptance_processes.positive: Compliance release can persist only when release spine, process runtime, evidence, payload, permission and audit preconditions pass.
acceptance_processes.negative: Missing evidence, missing advisor approval, inactive compliance step, audit failure, forbidden payload or admin override blocks release.

acceptance_processes.positive: Exception/audit handling exposes persisted action outcomes and exception state for review.
acceptance_processes.negative: Display-only audit rows, missing audit persistence or debug metadata cannot count as release proof.

acceptance_processes.positive: UI, service and tests state that compliance release controls client visibility but is not client acceptance.
acceptance_processes.negative: Released content cannot be labelled accepted by client, advice executed, downloaded/exported or client decisioned by compliance release alone.

stop_if_needed: implementation requires a new API route
release is mapped to client acceptance
audit failure cannot be represented fail-closed
